scores.proposal: The Arc — timeline-as-story with a participation ladder
scores.strengths: Best reader-journey engineering of the three: the timeline is readable end-to-end in ~6 minutes with zero clicks; time-proportional spacing makes the 2021–22 pileup and the 2023–24 silence physically felt; cliffhanger handoffs + the injected minimap stitch 14 pages into one continuous pull; the fast-path toggle operationalizes PAPERS.md's own 'three papers explain 80%' claim. The participation ladder is the only proposal that converts the site's stated goal (confidence to participate in AI creation) into information architecture — 14 escalating codas with device-local progress paying off at build.html as the 15th node ('2026: you'), with codas pointing at NavSha's own repos for an authentic voice. Shippability is strong: ~80% plumbing ported from two shipped siblings, 'all 14 pages at Tier-3 depth first' means the arc never shows a coming-soon hole, and papers.json forces the hard editorial decisions early. Technical risk is low: no build step, no live inference, baked JSON, click-to-activate facades, bbycroft link-out only. Risk list is honest and specific (including the not-yet-enabled Pages setup).
scores.weaknesses: Heaviest interlocked editorial load: 14 hooks + cliffhangers + honest-limits + codas, where a cliffhanger edit touches the timeline, the previous page's handoff, and the ladder simultaneously. Weakest visual identity of the three — acts and accent quads, but no ownable signature device; nothing gives the one-sentence hook a link-economy site needs. The homepage is rendered at runtime by timeline.js from fetched JSON, which weakens SEO/robustness of the primary artifact, and components.js-injected chrome is a nav single point of failure (mitigated but real). localStorage ladder is per-device only; fast-path flag mutating handoffs adds a subtle state dimension to test.

scores.strengths: Best solo-builder shippability, full stop: checkpoint 1 already produces a complete public artifact (one timeline page with all 14 as finished rich cards + participate.html), every later checkpoint is exactly one page plus one status-flag flip, and the 4 Tier-3 papers never need pages at all — the smallest total surface (~13 pages). The 'prose lives in HTML, never JS' hard rule is the most robust content architecture proposed. Uniquely concrete honesty beat: the Chinchilla-correction toggle in the flagship scaling widget, which protects the site's credibility where being wrong is fatal. Real discipline artifacts: checked-in templates/ kept out of docs/, spikes/ gating live-inference scope creep, per-widget asset budgets (≤3MB, webp), bump_css.py fixing the documented sibling papercut. Lowest technical risk of the three.
scores.weaknesses: Weakest reader experience and distinctiveness: the timeline is a rich card list, not a story — no felt time axis, no narrative gap captions, no handoffs between papers, so momentum depends entirely on prose quality per card. Tier-3 papers existing only as index anchors means no shareable per-paper pages or OG cards (acknowledged, but it truncates the link economy for 4 of 14 papers). participate.html is a generic 5-rung ladder rather than per-paper accumulating progress, so the 'confidence to participate' goal is served by one page instead of the whole IA. 'Go deeper →' affordances risk making the site read as an annotated index rather than a journey. Nothing here produces a memorable one-sentence description.

scores.proposal: The Descent — fourteen papers, one curve (2012–2025)
scores.strengths: Strongest design distinctiveness and share hook by far: 'thirteen years of AI history drawn as one descending loss curve' is a genuinely ownable conceit where spacing is data, the 2023–24 gap is annotated content, and the unlabeled 15th node is the narrative payoff. The cream paper-chip inversion (when the paper speaks, you see the paper) is a cheap, semantic, screenshot-friendly craft signature in the Distill lineage. Best link-economy thinking: per-paper OG cards, a colophon page for skeptical HN readers, copy-citation buttons. Widget specs are the most crafted of the three (capability-vintage readout + compute-optimal lock + '$ odometer' on scaling; SVG attention arcs with heatmap toggle; latent-vs-pixel diffusion toggle; read/run/tweak/train coda tags with honest time estimates). Mitigations are unusually specific (spine measured from DOM via ResizeObserver + document.fonts.ready, never hardcoded; static-drawn fallback; svh units; one rAF write per frame).
scores.weaknesses: Highest technical risk, concentrated in the signature: a scroll-drawn SVG threaded through variable-height, lazily-booted content is fragile under font reflow, resize, and iOS Safari — and if the signature janks, the whole conceit janks. Scope is self-admittedly 3–4x taylor-transformer for a solo builder (4 widgets + 6 scrollies + 4 plates + the spine + dark-only art direction). Four type families is one too many by its own admission; two color systems plus a temperature ramp risk visual mud. Dark-only is a bet against long-form reading comfort. The participation story is weaker than The Arc's: door.html aggregates codas but nothing accumulates — no progress, no per-paper rungs feeding back to the timeline.

winner: The Arc — timeline-as-story with a participation ladder
synthesis:
BASE: The Arc, adopted as written — with the grafts below. Zero-build static site, GitHub Pages from /docs on main (.nojekyll, all internal URLs relative for the /ml-papers/ subpath; enabling Pages is a checkpoint-1 task). Routes: index.html (the timeline spine, the primary artifact), papers/{slug}.html × 14, build.html ("2026: you" — the 15th node), colophon.html (replaces about.html — see Graft 3), 404.html. One stylesheet (css/site.css?v=N) with :root tokens; native ES modules with the taylor-transformer widget contract (async init(panel) → optional (stepIndex)=>{} for scroll.js); lazy boot via IntersectionObserver (rootMargin 600px) + dynamic import() + pendingSteps buffer + graceful per-widget failure; generation-counter cancellation in every async widget; scroll.js ported verbatim. papers.json is the single source of truth for slug, title, year/month, arXiv, act, category, tier, minutes, hook, consequence, cliffhanger, coda, and a status field (see Graft 5). No live ML inference in v1 (live ONNX is a v2 cut-line requiring a spike — see Graft 7). Embeds: Polo Club Transformer Explainer and Diffusion Explainer only (MIT), always click-to-activate facades with committed poster screenshots, ≥1200px viewport gate, link-out below; bbycroft.net/llm is a screenshot teaser that links out only — never iframe, never fork. Keep from The Arc unchanged: five acts with era header bands and narrative gap captions; time-proportional node spacing (capped); the fast-path toggle (Transformer → Scaling Laws → RLHF, localStorage flag that re-routes handoffs); the minimap strip on every paper page; the rigid paper-page anatomy (minimap → hook block → aha within one scroll → what-it-changed → honest-limits → build coda → cliffhanger handoff); the 14-rung participation ladder with localStorage progress labeled "built on this device," checkmarks surfacing on timeline nodes, paying off on build.html grouped by layer-of-the-stack, with coda links into NavSha's own repos (taylor-transformer, dogs-vs-cats, tiny-llm-pipeline); mobile inversion below 780px (sticky visual docks top ≤48svh, steps always-visible, tap-first, 44px targets); tools/bump_css.py, tools/check_links.py + the 5-line GitHub Action; DESIGN.md-first governance with the CLAUDE.md read-before-visual-work rule; reciprocal interactive-explainers banners.

GRAFT 1 (from The Descent) — the loss-curve conceit becomes the spine's visual device. The Arc's timeline rail is drawn as one continuous descending SVG curve — history as a training run — from 2012 (high) to 2025 (low), continuing past DeepSeek-R1 to the unlabeled 15th node that feeds build.html. Implementation: js/spine.js measures node DOM positions (ResizeObserver + document.fonts.ready) and generates the path from measurements — never hardcoded; scroll drives stroke-dashoffset with exactly one rAF write per frame; all heights affecting node position are reserved before lazy boot. Risk containment: prefers-reduced-motion or a perf fallback renders the curve fully drawn and static; below the phone breakpoint the curve moves to a left rail (git-graph idiom) with a --time-scale token halved so 2012–2017 doesn't cost eight screens. spine.js also renders the horizontal mini-spine variant used as the paper-page minimap (current node lit). The hero and OG description carry the one-sentence hook: "Thirteen years of AI, drawn as a single descending loss curve."

GRAFT 2 (from The Descent) — paper chips. A .paper-chip component: a cream, light-on-dark inverted card used whenever the paper itself speaks — the citation header on every paper page (authors, venue, arXiv link, copy-citation button), short attributed quotes, and redrawn-figure captions. Set in a system serif stack (Charter/Georgia — do NOT add STIX; the inversion is the device, not the font), with a mono arXiv-ID tab. Font budget: three families total (display serif, Inter for narration/UI, one mono), locked in DESIGN.md.

GRAFT 3 (from The Descent) — link-economy chrome. Per-paper OG cards at docs/og/og-{slug}.png, injected by tools/inject_meta.py reading papers.json. about.html becomes colophon.html: how every figure was redrawn from data (never copied), provenance of every baked JSON (which baker, which model, when), embed attributions and licenses, prices.json date stamps, the design system in brief. 404.html: "a node that fell off the curve."

GRAFT 4 (from The Descent) — widget craft specs, adopted verbatim into the build spec: SCALING LAWS: three coupled sliders (params/data/compute) with a compute-optimal lock, animated log-log frontier on charts.js, and three live readouts — loss → capability vintage ("≈ GPT-2, 2019"), training-$ odometer count-up, and a "wait for the next model?" verdict line. ATTENTION: primary rendering is SVG attention arcs sweeping over token chips (heatmap grid as an engineer's toggle), ~8 curated sentences each with a "why this sentence" note (coreference, negation, long-range dependency), baked offline from GPT-2 small. DIFFUSION: denoising scrubber over baked webp strips plus a latent-vs-pixel toggle showing the 64×64 latent beside the decoded frame. CODAS: 2–4 rungs per paper, each tagged read/run/tweak/train with an honest time estimate ("clone nanoGPT and read model.py — 2 hrs").

GRAFT 5 (from The Deepening Timeline) — checkpoint discipline. Checkpoint 0: DESIGN.md via /design-consultation + tokens + scaffold + templates. Checkpoint 1 = deployable v1: index.html with all 14 nodes as complete self-sufficient cards on the drawn spine; all 14 paper pages at plate grade (header chip, lede, one redrawn diagram, lesson-that-outlived-it, coda, handoff — no widgets yet; the 4 Tier-3 pages are FINAL at this grade); build.html; colophon.html; Pages enabled (main, /docs, .nojekyll). From checkpoint 2 on, each checkpoint ships exactly one artifact and flips one status field in papers.json ('plate'|'live'): (2) Scaling Laws flagship, (3) Chain-of-Thought, (4) Transformer/Attention, (5) Stable Diffusion, (6–11) the six Tier-2 scrolly upgrades best-first: RLHF, DeepSeek-R1, GPT-2, GPT-3, MoE, Constitutional AI. The site is complete-feeling at every checkpoint; cutting the project at any point leaves a coherent artifact.

GRAFT 6 (from The Deepening Timeline) — content robustness rules. Hard rule: prose lives in HTML, never in JS. index.html node cards are static HTML generated at EDIT time, not runtime: tools/build_timeline.py reads papers.json and idempotently rewrites a marked block in index.html (same pattern as inject_meta.py) — so the primary artifact is fully readable with JS off and SEO-complete, while papers.json remains the single source of truth for hooks/cliffhangers/codas (which repeat across the timeline, the previous page's handoff, and build.html). timeline.js/spine.js are pure enhancement (curve draw, act progress, fast-path highlight, ladder checkmarks). components.js injects only repeated chrome (minimap, coda shell, prev/next handoff) and every placeholder contains a hard-coded static fallback (at minimum a "← timeline" anchor and static coda text).

GRAFT 7 (from The Deepening Timeline) — honesty and discipline artifacts. The Chinchilla-correction toggle/callout ships in the scaling-laws widget from day one — non-negotiable; the flagship being quietly wrong is fatal to credibility. templates/paper-tier1.html and templates/paper-tier2.html live outside docs/ (never deployed) with the scrolly markup contract, meta block, coda section, and nav fallbacks pre-wired. spikes/ directory with the rule: any live in-browser inference work requires a completed spike there first, and the precomputed version must already be shipped. Asset budget: ≤3MB per widget data directory, webp only, lazy-loaded, exceptions documented in DESIGN.md. prices.json isolated and date-stamped with a visible "prices as of" line in the CoT and scaling widgets.

COLOR SYSTEM (resolving Arc-acts vs Descent-categories): dark graphite ground (neutral near-black, between the siblings' warm and cool blacks). Position on the spine is owned by chronology; COLOR is owned by the five PAPERS.md categories as accent quads (base/deep/light/gradient) remapped onto one base family via body[data-cat] — so the 2021 cluster honestly reads as four threads firing at once, and cross-thread cliffhangers ("meanwhile, in vision…") match node colors. The five acts own only the era header bands and gap captions. The Descent's temperature ramp is deferred to a post-checkpoint-11 polish task, and only if WCAG AA contrast survives per accent-on-ground pair.

FILE STRUCTURE: The Arc's tree as written, plus: docs/og/ (per-paper cards), docs/papers/_template references replaced by top-level templates/, spikes/, js/spine.js, tools/build_timeline.py, tools/bake_paper_data.py (redraws paper figures as data for plates and Tier-2 charts), and colophon.html in place of about.html.
